goal: Add a layered test verification workflow that provides under-1-minute fast feedback and reorganizes ordinary PR/verify toward a 5-minute target without weakening quality contracts.
non_goals: Deleting tests merely to reduce runtime
Weakening coverage baselines or thresholds without explicit approval
Building a full expansion-ready test impact graph in first slice
Replacing bun run verify as PR readiness entrypoint
Bypassing same-area test, quarantine, persistence, or release requirements

success_criteria.criterion: Routine local changes can run a fast lane in seconds to under 1 minute
Ordinary PR/verify has credible path to under 5 minutes for non-high-risk changes
Fast-lane routing is explainable via changed files and risk notes
High-risk paths still trigger heavier checks (E2E, live-provider, release gates)
Reports and output clearly distinguish fast feedback from PR readiness
Regression tests prove lane routing does not skip required checks

must_preserve_invariants: bun run verify remains the PR readiness entrypoint
Coverage baselines and thresholds are not weakened without approval
Fast-lane success is NOT presented as merge-ready
allowed_optimization_scope: Refine lane selection logic in quality-gate/runner.ts
Add fast-lane mode definition in quality-gate/modes.ts
Extend impact-report.ts to emit fast-lane signals
Optimize test selection without deleting high-signal tests
Add conditional escalation for coverage and heavy evidence
stop_and_reopen_conditions: Implementation weakens quality contracts (coverage, same-area, quarantine, release)
Fast-lane success is presented as PR readiness
Implementation ignores existing gate architecture and creates unrelated parallel system
Either fast lane or PR/verify layering is dropped

deferred_soft_unknowns.risk: Must decide whether coverage remains mandatory, becomes changed-line-first, or is conditionally escalated
